frameworks.name: reinforcement learning (belief-state POMDP TDRL)
frameworks.one_line: TDRL with belief-state over ambiguous stimuli computes confidence-weighted reward prediction errors

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: psychometric SDT framework cannot by itself explain confidence-dependent trial-by-trial choice updating

frameworks.name: Bayesian observer
frameworks.one_line: Bayesian classifier with statistical decision confidence as a teaching signal

frameworks.name: win-stay/lose-shift heuristic
frameworks.one_line: simple repetition heuristic predicts difficulty-independent bias, contrary to the data

core_question: Do past rewards bias subsequent perceptual decisions in a way that depends on the confidence of the previous decision?
key_assumptions: well-trained subjects have reached asymptotic psychometric performance (low bias, low lapse)
perceptual ambiguity can be represented as a belief state, a probability distribution over states
reward prediction error should scale with decision confidence under POMDP-TDRL
manipulations: difficulty of the previous trial (stimulus mixture / sensory evidence strength)
outcome of the previous trial (rewarded vs unrewarded)
sensory modality across datasets (olfaction, audition, vision)
key_findings: past rewards bias next choices specifically when the previous trial was difficult and confidence was low
this confidence-dependent updating effect generalises across six datasets, four labs, three species, and multiple modalities
effect survives correction for slow drifts in side bias via a t-1 / t+1 subtraction procedure
belief-state TDRL reproduces the pattern; stimulus-blind TDRL and win-stay/lose-shift do not
interpretations: reinforcement learning mechanisms remain continuously engaged even after perceptual task acquisition is complete
statistical decision confidence gates the magnitude of reward-driven choice updating, optimising behaviour under sensory uncertainty
